title: Peace Be With You
subtitle: The risen Lord steps through closed doors and stands in the midst of his frightened disciples

stillness: Be still now, where you are gathered.
The doors are shut. Let the senses be shut too.
Wait for the One you are waiting for.
gospel: And rising that same hour, the two disciples went back to Jerusalem,
and found the eleven gathered together, and those who were with them,
saying: The Lord is risen indeed, and has appeared to Simon.
And while they were speaking of these things,
when it was now late, on that first day of the week,
and the doors were shut where the disciples were gathered for fear of the Jews,
Jesus came and stood in the midst of them,
and said to them: Peace be with you.
I myself: it is I. Do not be afraid.
behold: See here the diligence of the disciples, and the fervor of their faith.
Their heart was burning with the fire of the Lord's words.
This is the day of good news; if we keep silent, we shall be charged with guilt.
Consider now how great was the gladness there,
while these and those told one another their joys.
But Thomas had gone out from among them.
And the doors being shut — for fear of the Jews —
the Lord Jesus came to his own, clothed in the highest garments of his risen glory,
and entering through the closed doors, he stood in the midst, that he might be seen by all.
He stood in the midst: as the sun among the stars, to give them light;
as the lily among lilies, to adorn them;
as the prince among his soldiers, to make them strong;
as the master among his disciples, to teach them;
as a father among his sons, to bring them to one heart;
as the heart among the members, to give them life;
and as the common friend, that he might give himself to them all.
consider: When the Savior came, they were in fear, it was late, they were gathered into one, and the doors were shut.
In these four, four things are shown us, needful to receive the Savior we await.
The first is fear, which cleanses the house of conscience: the fear of the Lord drives out sin.
The second is contempt of vainglory — for it was now late, and the proud light of the world had hidden itself.
The third is unity, for they were gathered in love; the Savior does not visit men who are at odds.
The fourth is stability, that one remain in the house of conscience, not wandering curiously without — so the doors of the senses must be shut.
He gives the peace he came to give: not the peace of time, for they were to be laid open to many tribulations,
but the peace of the heart, and the peace of eternity — the one now, the other to come.
And rising in beauty from the dead, he first commended peace to his disciples,
to show that those come to the sight of him who keep peace and concord in heart and body.
From which it is plain: they are the peacemakers who are truly the disciples of Christ.
prayer: Lord Jesus, you who came in through the closed doors,
stand now in the midst of us.
You said to your own: Peace be with you —
not the peace of time, but the peace of the heart and the peace of eternity.
Peace I leave with you, my own peace I give to you.
It is I; do not be afraid.
Where two or three are gathered in your name, there you are in the midst of them.
Stand, then, in the midst of me, as the sun among the stars,
that you may give me light, and adorn me, and make me strong, and give me life.

latinJewel.latin: Stetit in medio discipulorum: sicut sol inter sidera, ut eos illuminaret; sicut lilium inter lilia, ut eos decoraret; sicut princeps inter milites, ut eos roboraret; sicut pater inter filios, ut eos concordaret.
latinJewel.english: He stood in the midst of the disciples: as the sun among the stars, to give them light; as the lily among lilies, to adorn them; as the prince among his soldiers, to make them strong; as a father among his sons, to bring them to one heart.
